# BRIEF

## User-Facing Goal

When someone visits a project URL, they should see the hosted images clearly and simply. If the page is unavailable, they should get a polished explanation and a path back to HostingQr.

## Success Criteria

- Public slug pages use real backend data.
- Saved images render in a single column with small vertical spacing.
- Images take as much of the page width as practical.
- Disabled projects show a branded unavailable state.
- Missing projects show a branded not-found state.

## UX Notes

- Keep the live page minimal and reading-focused.
- Unavailable states should feel intentional, not broken.
- Redirect visitors naturally back to the homepage/product.

## Risks Or Tradeoffs

- Public state messaging needs to distinguish disabled vs missing without leaking unnecessary details.
